answer:
**Iqbal's poem "Insaan" ("Man" or "Human") is chapter 14 of *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*.**[^1]

The poem's central question about humanity revolves around **the enigma of human existence**: Why has Creation made humanity a profound mystery to itself—restless with an unquenchable thirst for self-knowledge, yet forever barred from unfolding life's secrets—unlike the rest of nature, which finds joy and fulfillment in its destined role? This is starkly contrasted with natural elements (rivers, clouds, stars, sun) that move purposefully and contentedly, while humanity alone bears unshared sorrow.[^2][^3][^4]

Key opening lines frame this:

> What a strange tyranny of Creation's art,  
> To make of Man an **Enigma apart**,  
> And hide that Enigma from his own gaze.[^5]  
>   
> He is restless with a thirst for knowing,  
> But the **mystery of life will not unfold**.[^6]

The poem closes by lamenting this isolation: "There is no one to share the sorrow of Man; / How **bitter is the existence of Man**!"[^7]

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch. 14: [Insaan](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/).
[^2]: Ibid., [p-ed0bbf](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-ed0bbf).
[^3]: Ibid., [p-ad335e](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-ad335e) to [p-edc3e3](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-edc3e3).
[^4]: Ibid., [p-18d11b](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-18d11b).
[^5]: Ibid., [p-d7ec8e](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-d7ec8e).
[^6]: Ibid., [p-3a62d8](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-3a62d8).
[^7]: Ibid., [p-62d764](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/14-insaan/translation/#p-62d764).
